title: eremophila waitii
intro: Eremophila waitii, also known as 'Western Desert Emu Bush', is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Indigenous Australian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eremophila waitii include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in the treatment of colds and respiratory issues. Eremophila waitii is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and can also be used as a topical cream or ointment to harness its medicinal properties.
